title: (P75) Isolation of single nuclei from frozen tissues
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ci4hb","text":"This protocol details the preparation of cryopreserved samples for single-nucleus RNA sequencing (snRNA-seq) and the subsequent storage of extracted nuclei for future use. This workflow is particularly advantageous when fresh tissue access is limited or when longitudinal study designs require the reuse of the same nuclei batches. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: For vertebrates including mice, voles, lizards, frogs, and fish: Prepare 10X ACSF by following the table below. A B C 1 Reagent Final concentration (mM) Amount (g) 2 KCl 25 1.864 3 NaH2PO4 12 1.44 4 HEPES 200 47.66 5 Sodium ascorbate 50 9.9 6 Thiourea 20 1.522 7 Sodium pyruvate 30 3.3 8 CaCl2 10 1.11 9 1 M MgCl2 13 13.17 mL 10 100 mM Taurine 0.1 1 mL 11 Myo inositol 30 5.4 12 NMDG 960 187.4 13 35% HCl n/a pH to 7.3 14 H2O n/a To 1 L 15 Total n/a 1 L Note: Store the 10X ACSF in 20 mL/tube aliquot at -20C. The thiourea turns yellow when ACSF expires. For invertebrate, fruit fly: Prepare 10X AHL by following the table below. %s %s Note: Store the 10X AHL in 20 mL/tube aliquot at -20C. The thiourea turns yellow when AHL expires.
Prepare 100µM TTX by following the table below. %s %s Note: Aliquot into 200 µL each, store at -20C.
Prepare 100 mM DNQX by following the table below. %s %s Note: Aliquot into 200 µL each, store at -20C.
Prepare 25 mM APV by following the table below. %s %s Note: Aliquot into 200 µL each, store at -20C.
Prepare 100X Kynurenic acid solution by following the table below. %s %s Note: Aliquot into 2mL each, store at -20C.
Prepare 10% BSA by following the table below. %s %s Note: This solution can be prepared in advance and stored at -20C for 1 year.
Prepare 100 mM taurine by following the table below. %s %s Note: This solution can be prepared in advance and stored at -20C for 1 year.
Prepare 50 mL NIM1 by following the table below. %s %s Note: This solution can be prepared in advance and stored at 4C for 1 month.
Proceed to 10X droplet generation.

Sort 24000 cells using 70µm nozzle and lowest sample pressure (=43.3 µl) into 18.8 RT mix. For bigger nuclei (e.g. frog), 18000 nuclei using 100µm nozzle (= 43.3 µl) into 18.8 RT mix. CRITICAL: One should try to use the lowest possible sorting pressure possible to avoid breaking the nuclei during the sorting.
Wet a 1.5 ml tube with 18.8 µI RT reagent B.
Briefly examine quality by mixing with propidium iodide and trypan blue.
Filter through 40 µm filter.
Add 0.5 µL 1000X SiR-DNA or Draq5.
Resuspend in 500 µL staining buffer
Discard supernatant.
Centrifuge 400g, 4C, 5min.
Add 800 µL staining buffer.
4C incubate 20 minutes.
Add 1 µL NeuN antibody.
Resuspend in 200 µL staining buffer.
Discard supernatant.
Centrifuge 400g, 4C, 5min.
Add 1000 µL staining buffer and gently mix by slowly pipetting up and down.
Thaw the DMSO stored samples on ice.
Prepare nuclei staining buffer by following the table below. %s %s
Store the aliquots at -80C.
Aliquot into 10-20 tubes depending on the need.

Resuspend the pellet into 1mL nuclei freezing medium. Optional: check the nuclei quality and quantity here. Good quality nuclei should look round, with minimal visible tissue debris, without small membranes, and abundant in quantity. Most if not all of the nuclei should be stained with cell nonpermeable dyes.
Centrifuge 400g, 4C, 5min.
Carefully lay the cell lysate on the top of the cushion.
Filter through 40 µm filter.
Filter through 70 µm filter.
On ice 1 minute.
Use the NIM3 with tritonx100 to slowly resuspend pellet by pipetting up and down 3 times.
Add 10 µL 10% Triton X-100 to 1mL NIM3, mix gently to avoid bubbles.
Transfer the 1 mL 1%BSA cushion into a 5mL polypropylene flow tube.
Add 100 µL of 10% BSA into 900 µL of NIM3 to make 1%BSA cushion.
Remove supernatant.
Centrifuge 400g, 4C, 5min.

Dounce on ice with tight pestle 5 times. CRITICAL: For tissue type used the first time, the number of Dounce should be determined by a few trial experiment. Optimal number is determined by generating the largest amount of nuclei while maintaining the integrity of the nuclei morphology.
Dounce on ice with loose pestle 10 times,

Transfer the dissected tissue into the Dounce on ice. Critical: the amount of tissue should be limited. Too much tissue at this step will result in failed extraction. A general starting point is 50mg in weight or 2mm in size.
Dissect the region out while the section is frozen under a microscope.
Keep the section cold and place the section onto dry ice plate.
Cryosection the O.C.T. block into 200 um sections.
Add 1mL NIM3 to a Dounce.
Place the Dounce homogenizer (Dounce for short from here on) on ice.
Prepare nuclei freezing medium by following the table below. %s %s
Prepare NIM3 by following the table below. %s %s Note: 1 mL of NIM3 is used per sample.
Prepare NIM2 by following the table below. %s %s
Store the snap frozen tissue under -80C.
Wait until the O.C.T. block completely freezes.
Place the O.C.T. block on the PCR rack. Critical: Placing the O.C.T. block directly into the liquid nitrogen will break the block.
Float a PCR rack on liquid nitrogen.
Embed into O.C.T..
Dissect the brain in ice cold ACSF/AHL prepared in step 12.
Sacrifice the animal using CO2 and cervical dislocation.
Add ddH2O to 200mL. Note: the prepared solution is kept on ice with continuous oxygenation through the entire experiment.
For vertebrates including mice, voles, lizards, frogs, and fish: Add 6mL 7.5% NaHCO 3 . For invertebrates, fruit fly: Add 896 µL 7.5% NaHCO 3 .
Oxygenate the solution with carbogen (95% O₂, 5% CO₂) for 5 minutes prior to the next step, maintaining continuous bubbling thereafter.
Place the solution on ice.

For vertebrates including mice, voles, lizards, frogs, and fish: Prepare 1X ACSF by following the table below in order. %s %s CRITICAL: This solution needs to be prepared fresh. The pH should be arround 7.3. Osmolarity should be arround 300-310 mOsm. For invertebrates, fruit fly: Prepare 1X AHL by following the table below in order. %s %s CRITICAL: This solution needs to be prepared fresh. The pH should be arround 7.5. Osmolarity should be arround 260-280 mOsm.
creator: Yunming Wu
